user_query:
You're acting as my startup operator. We're building a B2B SaaS for team health analytics used by engineering managers. I need a stage-aware strategy and concrete next steps.

Please do the following:
1) Read all files under input/:
   - input/metrics.csv (cohort retention and funnel metrics)
   - input/interviews.jsonl (summaries of recent customer interviews)
   - input/backlog.json (our current feature/experiment backlog)
   - input/budget.yaml (runway, headcount, and spend constraints)
   - input/company_brief.md (one-page overview of our product and target segment)

2) Diagnose whether we are pre- or post-PMF based on evidence from the files and answer the three core retention questions. Then synthesize the implications into a focused plan. Write to output/strategy/diagnosis.json using this schema:
   {
     "stage": "pre-PMF" | "post-PMF",
     "retention_questions": {
       "returning_users": true|false,
       "upset_if_gone": true|false,
       "word_of_mouth": true|false
     },
     "justification": "Short evidence-based rationale that cites the inputs you used",
     "computed_metrics": {
       "waus": number,
       "day7_retention": number,
       "month1_retention": number
     },
     "priorities": [
       {"title": "string", "owner": "product|engineering|design|marketing|finance|legal|sales", "time_cost": "brief note on founder time cost"}
     ],
     "decision_routing": {
       "reversible": [{"title": "string", "owner": "function"}],
       "irreversible": [{"title": "string", "owner": "function"}]
     },
     "traps_flagged": ["string"],
     "data_sources": ["list the input/ paths you used"]
   }

3) Draft a 90-day action plan that is stage-appropriate, week-by-week (or sprint-by-sprint), explicitly accounts for founders' time cost, and emphasizes manual-first approaches with clear thresholds for when to automate. Save as output/plan/90_day_plan.md.

4) Create a cross-functional spawn plan that maps the near-term tasks to functional owners (product, engineering, design, marketing, finance, legal, sales). Each task should list all relevant functions it needs. Save as output/plan/spawn_plan.json with schema: {"tasks": [{"title": "string", "functions": ["product|engineering|design|marketing|finance|legal|sales"]}]}.

5) Maintain a decision log that classifies upcoming decisions into "Reversible" and "Irreversible" sections, names an owner for each, and lists the next step for how to proceed (e.g., lightweight test vs. scenario modeling). Save as output/decisions/decision_log.md.

Please keep recommendations scrappy and realistic given constraints, flag any common startup traps you see (with evidence), and make sure the files above are created exactly at those paths.
